food_subgroup: Shaved ice dessert — ultra-fine shaved milk ice topped with sweetened red bean (pat), mochi rice cake pieces, condensed milk, and ice cream

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no dedicated concept for Bingsu or Patbingsu. Closest parent concept is a generic shaved-ice dessert. No URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Korean shaved milk ice dessert with sweetened red bean paste, mochi pieces, condensed milk, and ice cream (patbingsu)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0CZC is the closest FoodEx2 parent for shaved ice / frozen dessert composites; no exact Bingsu code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Bingsu or Patbingsu as of April 2026.

allergens: gluten (mochi / rice cake may contain wheat starch)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard café serving of Bingsu is one bowl weighing approximately 450 g, typically shared between one to two people.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy: Sweetened condensed milk (FDC ID 170859), Vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 167574), Mochi / glutinous rice cake (FDC ID 168872), Adzuki beans cooked (FDC ID 175196); component-weighted composite for Patbingsu
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Patbingsu as a composite dish. Nutrient values are derived by component-weighted averaging of approved USDA SR Legacy entries for the four primary ingredients (shaved milk ice base approximated as whole milk, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla ice cream, cooked adzuki beans, and glutinous rice cake / mochi) in proportions consistent with a standard 450 g Patbingsu bowl. Values are cross-referenced against the Singapore HPB Foodo database for dessert-category plausibility. The input record cited the NIAS Korea Food Composition Database; however, NIAS is not on the Project Veritas approved-source list. All values have been re-anchored to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as the approved primary source. Values are consistent with the input record and are retained where they align with the USDA component derivation.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — analogous estimate for Patbingsu composite: condensed milk GI ~61, glutinous rice / mochi GI ~85–90, sweetened red bean GI ~29–33, vanilla ice cream GI ~37–61; weighted composite GI estimated at 62–68, midpoint 65
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Patbingsu exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 (medium) is a component-weighted analogous estimate using published GI values for the four primary ingredients: sweetened condensed milk (~61), glutinous rice cake / mochi (~85–90), sweetened adzuki red bean (~29–33), and vanilla ice cream (~37–61). Glycemic load per 450 g serving = (65 × 128.25 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 83.4, rounded to 83.5. This estimate is consistent with the input record and is documented as an analogous estimate per Project Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (42 mg per 100 g); modest saturated fat (2.2 g per 100 g) from dairy components
hypertension.guidance: Bingsu is low in sodium and poses minimal direct hypertension risk from salt; however, patients with hypertension and concurrent cardiovascular risk should limit portion size to 100–150 g to manage saturated fat and caloric intake from dairy toppings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load per standard serving (GL 83.5 for 450 g); high free sugar content (22.5 g per 100 g); mochi component has GI ~85–90
type2_diabetes.guidance: Bingsu is not suitable for patients with Type 2 Diabetes in standard portions — a full 450 g bowl delivers approximately 101 g of free sugars and a glycemic load of 83.5, far exceeding the WHO daily free sugar limit; if consumed, limit to 100–150 g shared between two to three people and choose fresh fruit toppings over condensed milk and sweetened red bean.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from dairy components (2.2 g per 100 g; ~9.9 g per 450 g serving); high sugar load contributing to triglyceride elevation risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat Bingsu as an occasional indulgence only — the saturated fat from condensed milk and ice cream (approximately 9.9 g per full bowl) and the very high sugar load (approximately 101 g per bowl) both contribute to dyslipidaemia risk; limit to a 100–150 g portion and prefer fresh fruit bingsu variants without condensed milk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very high free sugar content (22.5 g per 100 g) is pro-inflammatory; advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) from processed dairy and ice cream components
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Bingsu to small portions (100–150 g) on rare occasions; fresh fruit bingsu variants without condensed milk are lower in AGEs and free sugars, and the unsweetened red bean component is itself anti-inflammatory due to anthocyanins and dietary fibre.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bingsu (빙수) is Korea's iconic summer dessert — a dramatic, towering bowl of ultra-fine shaved ice topped with an array of sweet ingredients. The most traditional and beloved form is Patbingsu (팥빙수, literally 'red bean shaved ice'), in which the shaved ice is crowned with sweetened azuki red bean paste (pat), pieces of chewy mochi rice cake (tteok), a generous drizzle of sweetened condensed milk, and a scoop of vanilla ice cream. The shaved ice itself is made from frozen milk or flavoured milk shaved into silk-like, snow-textured flurries that melt instantly on the tongue. Modern café bingsu in Singapore and Korea has evolved into extravagant, photogenic desserts — matcha bingsu, strawberry bingsu, mango bingsu — with increasingly fine 'snow ice' textures. The toppings provide contrasting textures: chewiness from mochi, creaminess from ice cream, intense sweetness from condensed milk, and earthy depth from red bean. Nutritionally, Bingsu is a high-sugar, moderate-calorie dessert (155 kcal per 100 g) with a very high glycemic load per standard 450 g serving (GL ~83.5). It contains modest protein (3.5 g per 100 g) from dairy components, low sodium (42 mg per 100 g), and useful calcium (88 mg per 100 g). The high free sugar content (22.5 g per 100 g; ~101 g per full bowl) makes it unsuitable for patients with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing cardiovascular risk in standard portions. Bingsu is a treat food rather than an everyday food and is best enjoyed as an occasional dessert in small portions.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Patbingsu as a composite dish. Nutrient values are derived from a component-weighted average of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the primary ingredients (condensed milk, vanilla ice cream, mochi/glutinous rice cake, cooked adzuki beans, milk ice base). Values are consistent with the input record and plausible for the food category. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact approved-source match exists for the composite dish.
notes: 1. APPROVED SOURCE ANCHOR: The input record cited the NIAS Korea Food Composition Database, which is not on the Project Veritas v2 approved-source list. All nutrient values have been re-anchored to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (component-weighted composite) as the approved primary source. Values are consistent with the input record. 2. MONOUNSATURATED AND POLYUNSATURATED FAT: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1.0 — detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite dessert are not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of April 2026. 3. WATER: water_g set to -1.0 — moisture content for this composite shaved ice dessert is not reported as a single value in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for this preparation; the high water content of shaved ice is implicit but not quantified in the approved source. 4. TRANS FAT: trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for the component ingredients at typical proportions. 5. GI NOTE: GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values; no direct GI measurement for Patbingsu exists. 6. KOREAN DISH MULTILINGUAL RULE: Korean native-script name (팥빙수) is present per G6 requirement. Chinese, Malay, and Tamil names are also included given the Singapore café context of this dish. Hindi local name is not established for this Korean dessert in the Singapore context and is set to null. 7. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn URI is null — no dedicated FoodOn concept exists for Bingsu; gap note provided. FoodEx2 code A0CZC is used as closest parent for frozen/shaved ice dessert composites.
